whatThisIs: Two pregnancy-dating calculations in one tile. Naegele's rule estimates an estimated due date (EDD) from the first day of the last menstrual period (LMP + 280 days, or LMP - 3 months + 7 days). Robinson-Fleming uses first-trimester crown-rump length (CRL in mm) to compute gestational age in weeks and days, with a corresponding EDD. Sophie Well returns both, flags any discordance between LMP-based and CRL-based dating, and labels whether the discordance exceeds the ACOG redating thresholds (7 days in T1, 14 days in T2, 21 days in T3).
whenToUse: Use this at the new-OB intake visit and any subsequent visit where dating is in question - certainty of LMP is the central issue (irregular cycles, recent hormonal contraception, lactational amenorrhea, conception under fertility treatment all undermine LMP reliability). The CRL window is roughly 6-14 weeks; outside that window use second-trimester biometry (BPD, HC, FL, AC) instead, which this tile does not cover. The ACOG redating rule is a one-way ratchet: once an EDD has been established by the earliest available reliable ultrasound, it is not revised by later scans even if they disagree.
